title: Past Lives
logline: Twenty years after immigrating from Korea, Nora reconnects with her childhood sweetheart Hae Sung when he visits New York, forcing her to confront the life she might have lived versus the one she chose with her American husband Arthur.
antagonistOrConflict: The tension between past and present lives, cultural identity, and the paths not taken

actBreaks.description: Nora decides to reconnect with Hae Sung after 12 years, ending their online relationship and transitioning to her adult life with Arthur.

validation.ruling: Scene #46 shows Nora telling Hae Sung they need to stop talking, fundamentally changing the story direction from reconnection to separation and forcing both characters into new conflict states for the next act.

actBreaks.description: Hae Sung arrives in New York, bringing the past directly into Nora's present life and creating the central triangle.

validation.ruling: Scene #70 shows Hae Sung's arrival in New York during pouring rain, marking the transition into the final act where their long-distance relationship becomes a physical meeting with irreversible consequences.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality:
This script operates as a triptych examining three distinct time periods: childhood in Korea (scenes 2-12), young adulthood's digital reconnection (scenes 13-46), and the final confrontation in present-day New York (scenes 47-103). Each section spans roughly 12 years, creating a meditation on time's passage and identity's evolution. The structure is remarkably patient, allowing relationships to breathe and develop naturally rather than forcing dramatic beats. The circular framing device (scenes 1 and 98) provides elegant bookends that recontextualize the entire story as a moment observed by strangers, emphasizing themes of perspective and unknowability.

The script's genius lies in its restraint—major life transitions happen in time cuts rather than on-screen drama. We see the aftermath and adjustment rather than the crisis itself, creating a mature, reflective tone that mirrors how we actually experience life's pivotal moments in memory.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing follows an accordion structure: compressed childhood (scenes 2-12), extended digital courtship (scenes 13-46), and real-time present confrontation (scenes 47-103). The middle section uses rapid cross-cutting between Seoul and New York to build intimacy, while the final act slows to almost real-time as emotional stakes peak. Scene lengths average 1.3 pages but vary significantly—intimate conversations run longer while transitional moments are kept brief.

incitingIncident.description: Hungover Hae Sung wakes up, drinks water his mom left him, then grabs his phone and reads Nora's Facebook message asking if he remembers her.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. HAE SUNG'S ROOM ‑ SEOUL ‑ MOMENTS LATER

validation.ruling: Scene #19 shows Hae Sung drinking with friends but doesn't shatter the status quo or force action. Scene #22 is the true inciting incident where hungover Hae Sung reads Nora's Facebook message asking if he remembers her, shattering his ordinary world and establishing the main conflict that drives the entire story forward.

midpoint.description: Hae Sung waits nervously at Madison Square Park at sunrise until Nora appears, and they see each other for the first time in twenty years, both looking shocked as if seeing a ghost.
midpoint.sceneHeading: EXT. MADISON SQUARE PARK ‑ NEXT DAY ‑ SUNRISE

validation.ruling: Scene #76 perfectly captures the midpoint where Nora and Hae Sung see each other for the first time in twenty years at Madison Square Park. This moment shifts them from reactive communication to proactive meeting, raises stakes personally, and represents a point of no return in their relationship.

climax.description: Nora and Hae Sung walk silently to his uber pickup location and endure two excruciating minutes of heavy silence before the car arrives and they hug goodbye.
climax.sceneHeading: EXT. EAST VILLAGE STREET ‑ VERY LATE NIGHT ‑ ALMOST DAWN

validation.ruling: Scene #98 is setup conversation at the bar, not the climax confrontation. Scene #100 is the true climax where Nora and Hae Sung face their final goodbye with two excruciating minutes of heavy silence before his uber arrives - this is the peak emotional confrontation that resolves their story question and demonstrates their character arcs.

scenes.heading: INT. EAST VILLAGE BAR / SPEAKEASY ‑ NEW YORK ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: Three people sit in an East Village speakeasy booth with cocktails - Nora talks intensely with Hae Sung while Arthur silently scrolls his phone beside them.

scenes.heading: EXT. PYOUNGCHON ‑ SEOUL ‑ LATE‑90S ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Twelve-year-old Na Young walks home from school crying hard about getting second place in exams while Hae Sung walks alongside her with his basketball.

scenes.heading: INT. SEOUL FAMILY APARTMENT ‑ DAD'S OFFICE ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Na Young's parents pack up their offices while smoking and playing Leonard Cohen, as Na Young and Si Young announce their chosen American names - Michelle and Mary.

scenes.heading: INT. SEOUL FAMILY APARTMENT ‑ MOM'S OFFICE ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Mom packs her office while making immigration notes as Si Young draws on the computer and Na Young reads on the couch.

scenes.heading: INT. NA YOUNG AND HAE SUNG'S CLASSROOM ‑ ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Hae Sung leans against Na Young's desk at school while she draws flowers on his arm with a ballpoint pen, showing their easy intimacy.

scenes.heading: INT. MOM'S OFFICE ‑ NA YOUNG'S FAMILY APARTMENT ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Na Young tells her mom she likes Hae Sung because he's manly and that she'll probably marry him, while mom examines their new Korean passports.

scenes.heading: EXT. GWACHEON NATIONAL MUSEUM OF MODERN CONTEMPORARY ART ‑
scenes.summary: Na Young and Hae Sung visit a sculpture garden where they imitate the Singing Man sculpture while their mothers watch and discuss how well the children look together.

scenes.heading: EXT. GWACHEON NATIONAL MUSEUM OF MODERN CONTEMPORARY ART ‑
scenes.summary: Na Young and Hae Sung approach another sculpture of two faces gazing at each other, and Hae Sung looks at Na Young studying her face.

scenes.heading: INT. NA YOUNG'S MOM'S CAR ‑ EVENING
scenes.summary: In the backseat of a car, Na Young sleeps with her head on Hae Sung's lap while their hands are tangled together as he watches her.

scenes.heading: INT. NA YOUNG AND HAE SUNG'S CLASSROOM ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: On Na Young's last day of class before immigrating to Canada, she tells classmates she's never coming back while Hae Sung sits apart trying to ignore the conversation.

scenes.heading: EXT. NA YOUNG'S NEIGHBORHOOD ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Hae Sung walks Na Young home for the last time, maintaining an expressionless face and cold distance before calling out 'Hey!' to her at her apartment building.

scenes.heading: INT. PLANE TO TORONTO ‑ A FEW WEEKS LATER ‑ NIGHT
scenes.summary: On the plane to Toronto, the family practices English while Na Young and Si Young loop 'I'm fine. And you?' and laugh together.
scenes.turningPointNote: The family's physical departure from Korea to begin their new life in Canada

scenes.heading: INT. TORONTO PEARSON AIRPORT ‑ CANADA ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: The family arrives at Toronto Pearson Airport where the parents speak limited English to immigration officers while Nora sits on luggage and pets sleeping Michelle's hair.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S TORONTO CLASS ‑ MORNING
scenes.summary: Nora sits in her first Canadian classroom full of white kids, buzzing with nervousness and uncertainty as the bell rings to begin her new life.

scenes.heading: INT. FIELDS ‑ KOREA ‑ LUNCHTIME
scenes.summary: Hundreds of Korean men in military uniforms march through fields and later sit eating dinner from metal trays, including an anonymous Hae Sung among them.

scenes.heading: INT. NYC TAXI ‑ APPROACHING NYC ‑ DUSK
scenes.summary: Twenty-four-year-old Nora rides in a taxi from the airport toward Manhattan, staring in love with the gleaming city skyline view.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAD SCHOOL ‑ CLASSROOM ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: In grad school class, Nora sits between friends Janice and Rachel taking notes during feedback on her play pages while classmate Robert contributes to the discussion.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Nora talks with her mom on the phone while googling childhood friends from Seoul, finding lawyer Dong Yun and laughing about childhood memories of peeing her pants.

scenes.heading: INT. ALL‑NIGHT SOJU BAR ‑ SEOUL ‑ 3 A.M. KST
scenes.summary: Very drunk Hae Sung sits with grad student friends at 3 AM surrounded by empty soju bottles, consoling a friend over a breakup before discovering Nora's Facebook message.
scenes.turningPointNote: Hae Sung receives Nora's Facebook message asking if he remembers her, reconnecting them after 12 years

scenes.heading: EXT. STREETS OF SEOUL ‑ LATER, WEE HOURS OF THE MORNING
scenes.summary: Completely drunk Hae Sung stumbles alone through Seoul streets in the early morning dawn, struggling to walk straight but finding his way home.

scenes.heading: INT. HAE SUNG'S FAMILY APARTMENT ‑ SEOUL ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Hae Sung quietly unlocks his family apartment door and stumbles inside trying not to wake his parents in his inebriated state.

scenes.heading: INT. HAE SUNG'S ROOM ‑ SEOUL ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Hungover Hae Sung wakes up, drinks water his mom left him, then grabs his phone and reads Nora's Facebook message asking if he remembers her.

scenes.heading: INT. HAE SUNG'S KITCHEN ‑ SEOUL ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Hae Sung enters the kitchen where his mother has prepared traditional Korean hangover soup and sits down with his ordinary family for breakfast.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAD SCHOOL LIBRARY ‑ NEW YORK ‑ EVENING EST (MORNING
scenes.summary: Nora works at her laptop in the grad school library when she receives Hae Sung's punny response 'Of course' to her question, making her grin ear-to-ear.

scenes.heading: EXT. UPPER WEST SIDE STREETS ‑ NEW YORK ‑ MOMENTS LATER ‑
scenes.summary: Nora walks back to her dorm room with increasing briskness until she finds herself running and grinning as she sprints down New York streets.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM/HAE SUNG'S ROOM ‑ NEW YORK/SEOUL ‑
scenes.summary: Nora bursts into her dorm room and opens Skype, where she and Hae Sung video call for the first time, giggling uncontrollably like kids and marveling at seeing each other again.
scenes.turningPointNote: First video call between Nora and Hae Sung after 12 years, establishing their ongoing relationship

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM/HAE SUNG'S ROOM ‑ NEW YORK ‑ NIGHT EST
scenes.summary: Hours later on their video call, Hae Sung says he likes talking to Nora and must leave for class while she needs dinner, both reluctantly hanging up.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM ‑ NEW YORK ‑ NIGHT EST (DAY KST) ‑ TWO
scenes.summary: Two hours after their call, Nora brushes her teeth and creates a Korean keyboard reference to write Hae Sung a long email in broken Korean.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. SEOUL COFFEE SHOP/NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK ‑
scenes.summary: Hae Sung video calls Nora from a Seoul coffee shop while she's just woken up at 7 AM, laughing that she woke up early just to talk to him.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK/SEOUL BUS ‑ EVENING
scenes.summary: Dressed up Nora talks to Hae Sung who's on a Seoul bus heading to class, as he subtly shields his phone from other passengers.

scenes.heading: INT. HAE SUNG'S ROOM IN SEOUL/NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK ‑
scenes.summary: Hae Sung teases Nora about her rusty Korean during their video call, as she explains she only speaks Korean with him and her mother.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK/HAE SUNG'S ROOM IN SEOUL ‑
scenes.summary: During their video call, Hae Sung asks if Nora still wants to win the Nobel Prize like she said as a child, and she responds she's now interested in the Pulitzer.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK/HAE SUNG'S ROOM IN SEOUL ‑
scenes.summary: Hae Sung stares and smiles at Nora through the screen, telling her she's exactly like the 12-year-old in his memory - greedy and wanting everything.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK/HAE SUNG'S ROOM IN SEOUL ‑
scenes.summary: As Nora gets ready for class, she explains to Hae Sung about her upcoming artist residency in Montauk and asks if he's seen Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind.

scenes.heading: INT. HAE SUNG'S ROOM IN SEOUL ‑ LATE NIGHT KST
scenes.summary: Hae Sung watches Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind in his room.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAD SCHOOL ‑ CLASSROOM ‑ MORNING EST
scenes.summary: Nora writes a long email to Hae Sung instead of doing her school assignment while Janice and Rachel peek at her screen in the library.

scenes.heading: INT. HAE SUNG'S ROOM IN SEOUL/NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK ‑
scenes.summary: Nora asks Hae Sung why he's going to China to learn Mandarin and suggests he should come to New York to learn English instead.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM ‑ NEW YORK ‑ EVENING EST (MORNING KST)
scenes.summary: Nora asks into Skype if Hae Sung can hear her during a connection issue.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. SEOUL COFFEE SHOP ‑ MORNING KST (EVENING EST)
scenes.summary: Hae Sung patiently waits for his screen to load in a Seoul coffee shop as Nora's video is breaking up.

scenes.heading: INT. HAE SUNG'S ROOM ‑ SEOUL ‑ EVENING KST (MORNING EST)
scenes.summary: Hae Sung asks into Skype if Nora can hear him during their connection problems.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAD SCHOOL LIBRARY ‑ MORNING EST (EVENING KST)
scenes.summary: Nora impatiently waits in the grad school library for Hae Sung's video to load, saying hello in Korean.

scenes.heading: INT. SEOUL COFFEE SHOP ‑ MORNING KST (EVENING EST)
scenes.summary: Hae Sung patiently waits in a Seoul coffee shop for Nora's video to load for a very long time before the call finally fails.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM ‑ NEW YORK ‑ MORNING EST (EVENING KST)
scenes.summary: Sleeping Nora's Skype rings repeatedly as Hae Sung keeps calling, and she stirs but misses the call.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. SEOUL CABLE CAR/NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK ‑
scenes.summary: Hae Sung video calls Nora from a Seoul cable car to show her the beautiful city skyline, and she says she wishes she were there with him.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAE SUNG'S UNIVERSITY ‑ SEOUL ‑ AFTERNOON KST (LATE
scenes.summary: Hae Sung smokes with friends outside his university when he receives a message from Nora asking if they can talk.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK/HAE SUNG'S ROOM IN SEOUL ‑
scenes.summary: Nora asks Hae Sung when he could visit her in New York, and they realize the timing doesn't work - he needs a year and a half, she needs a year to visit Seoul.
scenes.turningPointNote: The logistics conversation that reveals their relationship cannot progress, leading to their decision to stop talking

scenes.heading: INT. HAMPTON JITNEY ‑ MONTAUK BOUND ‑ MORNING
scenes.summary: Nora naps curled up in her seat on the Hampton Jitney bus heading to Montauk, missing the beautiful New York City views outside the windows.

scenes.heading: EXT. MONTAUK RESIDENCY BARN HOUSE ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Nora arrives by cab at the rustic Montauk artist residency barn house where she will stay for a month.

scenes.heading: INT. MONTAUK RESIDENCY BARN HOUSE ‑ DOWNSTAIRS ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Nora enters the empty but charming residency house filled with art and books, then walks upstairs to find a room.

scenes.heading: INT. MONTAUK RESIDENCY BARN HOUSE ‑ UPSTAIRS ‑ CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Nora finds two unoccupied bedrooms across from each other upstairs, chooses the obviously nicer one, and walks into it.

scenes.heading: INT. BARN HOUSE ‑ NORA'S ROOM ‑ CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Nora enters her room at the barn house, sees previous artists' names on the wall, and adds 'Nora Moon' to the wall before unpacking.

scenes.heading: INT. SEOUL SUBWAY TRAIN ‑ LATE NIGHT KST (AFTERNOON EST)
scenes.summary: Hae Sung sits on a Seoul subway writing a long email to Nora, but deletes it before getting off the train.

scenes.heading: INT. ALL‑NIGHT SOJU BAR ‑ SEOUL ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: Hae Sung arrives at a soju bar where his friends ask about his upcoming trip to New York, and he downs all the glasses on the table instead of answering when they'll return.

scenes.heading: INT. BARN HOUSE ‑ NORA'S ROOM ‑ AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Arthur arrives at the barn house by cab while Nora naps, but we only see glimpses of him from a distance without seeing his face clearly.

scenes.heading: INT. BARN HOUSE ‑ NORA'S BEDROOM TO KITCHEN ‑ MIDDAY
scenes.summary: Nora wakes from her nap, notices Arthur's belongings in the room across from hers, then goes downstairs for coffee.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARN HOUSE PICNIC TABLE ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Nora and Arthur meet for the first time outside the barn house, introducing themselves and making small talk about his room.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARN HOUSE PICNIC TABLE ‑ NIGHT ‑ SOMETIME THAT SUMMER
scenes.summary: Nora explains the Korean concept of In-Yun (fate between people) to the other tipsy artists at the barn house.

scenes.heading: INT. PLANE TO SHANGHAI ‑ MORNING
scenes.summary: Hae Sung flies to Shanghai while Nora's voiceover continues explaining In-Yun and Buddhism.

scenes.heading: INT. SHANGHAI DORM ROOM ‑ DUSK
scenes.summary: Hae Sung sleeps in his small Shanghai dorm room with language exchange program materials on his bed as Nora's voiceover continues.

scenes.heading: EXT. SHANGHAI NIGHT MARKET ‑ NIGHT
scenes.summary: Hae Sung walks through a Shanghai night market with language exchange students, including a cute girl who glances at him while he smokes.

scenes.heading: INT. SHANGHAI NOODLE SHOP ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: The students eat noodles and drink at a Shanghai restaurant where Hae Sung makes eye contact and exchanges smiles with the cute girl.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARN HOUSE PICNIC TABLE ‑ NIGHT ‑ SOMETIME THAT SUMMER
scenes.summary: Nora finishes explaining In-Yun to Arthur at the barn house, suggesting they might have been somebody to each other in another lifetime.

scenes.heading: INT. TORONTO PEARSON AIRPORT ‑ A FEW DAYS LATER ‑ MORNING
scenes.summary: Nora and Arthur go through customs at Toronto airport as a couple, with Arthur answering questions about their trip to visit her family.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST VILLAGE STREET ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Nora and Arthur wait at a crosswalk in New York, make faces at each other, then walk away hand-in-hand as Nora reveals they're married.
scenes.turningPointNote: Reveals Nora and Arthur are married, establishing the central romantic triangle

scenes.heading: INT. REHEARSAL ROOM ‑ NEW YORK ‑ AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Nora watches a Korean-American actress perform a monologue from her play about crossing the Pacific Ocean during rehearsal.

scenes.heading: INT. BOOKSTORE ‑ BROOKLYN ‑ LATER THAT DAY
scenes.summary: Arthur signs copies of his novel 'Boner' at a bookstore while Nora brings him lunch and gives him a quick kiss.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA AND ARTHUR'S APT ‑ LIVING ROOM ‑ NEW YORK ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Nora and Arthur finish having sex and discuss food in Korean and English, deciding they want chicken wings.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST VILLAGE STREET ‑ LATER ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Nora and Arthur walk hand-in-hand as she mentions Hae Sung is coming to visit and Arthur asks why he's coming to New York.

scenes.heading: INT. ALL‑NIGHT SOJU BAR ‑ SEOUL ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: An older Hae Sung drinks with his longtime friends who ask if he's going to New York to see 'that girl' from his past.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW YORK CITY ‑ WHEN HAE SUNG ARRIVES
scenes.summary: Hae Sung arrives in New York City during a heavy rainstorm.

scenes.heading: INT. MIDTOWN HOTEL LOBBY ‑ NEW YORK ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Hae Sung struggles with his suitcase in the rain as New Yorkers bump into him, then enters the hotel lobby completely soaked.

scenes.heading: INT. MIDTOWN HOTEL ‑ HAE SUNG'S ROOM ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Hae Sung opens the curtains in his hotel room to see rain continuing to fall heavily outside.

scenes.heading: EXT. MIDTOWN HOTEL ‑ AWNING AT THE ENTRANCE ‑ LATER THAT DAY
scenes.summary: Hae Sung stands under the hotel awning trying to light a cigarette while watching people brave the weather with tourist umbrellas.

scenes.heading: INT. MIDTOWN HOTEL ‑ LOBBY ‑ EVENING
scenes.summary: Hae Sung gives up on sightseeing and lounges miserably in the hotel lobby watching TV and drinking as rain continues.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW YORK CITYSCAPES ‑ EVENING TO NIGHT
scenes.summary: The rain finally stops in New York City.

scenes.heading: EXT. MADISON SQUARE PARK ‑ NEXT DAY ‑ SUNRISE
scenes.summary: Hae Sung waits nervously at Madison Square Park at sunrise until Nora appears, and they see each other for the first time in twenty years, both looking shocked as if seeing a ghost.
scenes.turningPointNote: Nora and Hae Sung reunite after 20 years, the central encounter the entire story builds toward

scenes.heading: INT. MADISON SQUARE PARK SUBWAY STATION ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Nora and Hae Sung walk down to the subway station, wait for the train, board it, and leave.

scenes.heading: INT. SUBWAY ‑ R TRAIN ‑ LATER
scenes.summary: Nora and Hae Sung stand together holding the same subway pole without touching, awkwardly but sweetly smiling at each other.

scenes.heading: EXT. BROOKLYN BRIDGE PARK ‑ LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: At Brooklyn Bridge Park, Nora mentions her husband for the first time to Hae Sung, causing him to wince, and reveals he's taking a break from his girlfriend.

scenes.heading: EXT. BY THE WATER ‑ BROOKLYN BRIDGE PARK ‑ CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Nora and Hae Sung walk by the water surrounded by couples on dates, trying to ignore the lovers as they discuss their past relationships and dating history in this location.

scenes.heading: EXT. IN FRONT OF JANE'S CAROUSEL ‑ SOMETIME LATER ‑ SUNSET
scenes.summary: Sitting by Jane's Carousel at sunset, Nora asks Hae Sung why he looked for her 12 years ago, and he admits he wanted to see her one more time and was angry she just left.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA AND ARTHUR'S APARTMENT ‑ NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora returns home to find Arthur playing video games and tells him he was right that Hae Sung came to see her.

scenes.heading: INT. MIDTOWN HOTEL ‑ NIGHT
scenes.summary: Hae Sung returns alone to his small hotel room, takes off his shoes, and sits on the bed looking incredibly lonely.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA AND ARTHUR'S APARTMENT ‑ BATHROOM ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora talks to Arthur while getting ready for bed about how Korean Hae Sung is and how it makes her feel both not-Korean and more Korean, and Arthur asks if Hae Sung is attractive.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA AND ARTHUR'S APARTMENT ‑ BEDROOM ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Arthur admits he's mad but says he has no right to be, acknowledging Hae Sung flew thirteen hours and joking about Nora running away with him.

scenes.heading: INT. MIDTOWN HOTEL ‑ HAE SUNG'S ROOM ‑ SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Hae Sung gets out of the shower, gets into bed, and turns off the lights in his hotel room.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA AND ARTHUR'S APARTMENT ‑ BEDROOM ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: In bed, Arthur tells Nora he can't compete with her and Hae Sung's story, calling himself the evil white husband standing in the way of destiny, while questioning what would have happened if they'd met someone else first.

scenes.heading: EXT. BROOKLYN BRIDGE PARK ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: Brooklyn Bridge Park late at night is described as impossibly beautiful and the most romantic place in the world with couples making out.

scenes.heading: EXT. LINE FOR THE FERRY / SECURITY ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Hae Sung waits in line for the Statue of Liberty ferry and beams when Nora arrives, bringing her a breakfast bagel which she hungrily eats.

scenes.heading: EXT. ON THE FERRY ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Nora and Hae Sung board the ferry and stand at the railing together in an intolerably romantic setting, with Nora unable to make eye contact while Hae Sung looks at her openly.

scenes.heading: EXT. ON THE FERRY ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Nora takes tourist photos of Hae Sung with his phone, and they politely decline when a family offers to take a photo of them together.

scenes.heading: EXT. ON THE FERRY ‑ BACK OF THE STATUE OF LIBERTY ‑ MOMENTS
scenes.summary: From the ferry, Hae Sung and Nora see the Statue of Liberty from behind and Hae Sung observes that she's turned against them.

scenes.heading: EXT. ON THE FERRY ‑ HEADING BACK TO MANHATTAN ‑ AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: On the ferry back to Manhattan, Nora shows Hae Sung photos of her wedding where she wore a Korean hanbok, and he tells her she looks young in the photos.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST VILLAGE STREET ‑ LATER ‑ EVENING
scenes.summary: Walking to Nora's apartment, Hae Sung asks if Arthur knows he's coming over and who he is, and Nora confirms Arthur wants to meet him.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA AND ARTHUR'S APARTMENT ‑ NIGHT
scenes.summary: Arthur waits anxiously in their cleaned apartment until Nora and Hae Sung arrive, and he's startled by how well they seem to fit together beyond just sharing the same skin color.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST VILLAGE STREET ‑ NIGHT
scenes.summary: The three walk through East Village streets discussing the Statue of Liberty, with Arthur revealing he's never been there despite living in New York.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST VILLAGE RESTAURANT ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Arthur, Nora, and Hae Sung eat pasta at a bustling East Village restaurant, getting along wonderfully with Nora translating between them.

scenes.heading: INT. EAST VILLAGE BAR / SPEAKEASY ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: At the speakeasy from the opening scene, the three drink cocktails and bond over conversation about Hae Sung's military service, work, and cultural differences, while a young couple watches them curiously.
scenes.turningPointNote: This is the opening scene of the film, completing the narrative circle and establishing the central tension of the three-way dynamic

scenes.heading: INT. NORA AND ARTHUR'S APARTMENT ‑ CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Back at the apartment, Hae Sung picks up his bag to leave, exchanges pleasantries with Arthur about visiting Korea, then leaves with Nora to walk him to his uber.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST VILLAGE STREET ‑ VERY LATE NIGHT ‑ ALMOST DAWN
scenes.summary: Nora and Hae Sung walk silently to his uber pickup location and endure two excruciating minutes of heavy silence before the car arrives and they hug goodbye.

scenes.heading: EXT. NA YOUNG'S NEIGHBORHOOD ‑ PYOUNGCHON ‑ LATE‑90S ‑
scenes.summary: 12-year-old Na Young and Hae Sung stand together in front of her childhood apartment building in Korea, recreating their original goodbye but now in the soft glow of late night rather than afternoon.
scenes.turningPointNote: The film transitions into a dreamlike/magical realist moment that transforms the original goodbye into something transcendent

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST VILLAGE STREET ‑ 5 A.M. ‑ ALMOST DAWN
scenes.summary: Hae Sung asks Nora what if this life is also a past life and wonders who they'll be to each other in their next life, then gets in his uber and drives away while Nora stands alone and begins crying harder with each step home.
scenes.turningPointNote: The final goodbye between Nora and Hae Sung, with his philosophical question reframing their entire relationship and her emotional breakdown marking the definitive end

scenes.heading: INT. UBER ‑ DAWN
scenes.summary: Hae Sung sits alone in the uber's backseat looking out the window at New York waking up, faintly smiling as he feels both massive and small.
